description: LLM-as-judge scoring, long-text summarization, and embeddings on OCI GenAI.

tools.description:
Scores a candidate text against a rubric and returns a structured verdict (overall 0-10 score, per-criterion scores, rationale, pass/fail).

When to use: Use when the user asks to evaluate, grade, judge, or quality-gate a piece of generated text — e.g. checking an agent answer, a summary, an email draft, or a model output against named criteria.

How to communicate: Report the overall score, the pass/fail flag, and the top one or two weakest criteria in plain prose. Do not dump the entire raw JSON unless the user asks.

Example: user says 'score this support reply for tone, accuracy, and brevity'. Call {candidate:'Hi Jane, your refund of $42 was processed...', rubric:'tone (warm, professional), accuracy (matches order #1234), brevity (under 80 words)'} -> summarize: 'Overall 8.2/10, PASS. Weakest: brevity 6/10 (reply is 110 words).'

schema.name: candidate
schema.description: The text to evaluate. Pass the full candidate output as a single string, e.g. an agent answer, a draft email, or a model-generated summary. Long inputs beyond `max_candidate_chars` are truncated.
schema.default: Hi Jane, your refund of $42.00 for order #1234 was processed on 2026-06-15 and should appear on your card within 3-5 business days.
examples: Hi Jane, your refund of $42.00 for order #1234 was processed on 2026-06-15 and should appear on your card within 3-5 business days.
The capital of France is Paris, which has been the seat of French government since the late 10th century.

schema.name: rubric
schema.description: The scoring criteria as plain text. Either a comma-separated list of named criteria or a richer description with definitions, e.g. 'accuracy, completeness, tone' or 'tone (warm, professional), accuracy (matches order data), brevity (under 80 words)'. Truncated past `max_rubric_chars`.
examples: tone (warm, professional), accuracy (matches order #1234), brevity (under 80 words)
factual correctness, citation of sources, neutrality

model_provider.placeholder: Auto-set: cohere or generic based on model_id

_confDescriptions.model_id: OCI GenAI judge model ID. See .aidp/knowledge/oci-genai-models.md (e.g. cohere.command-a-03-2025, meta.llama-3-70b-instruct, xai.grok-4).
_confDescriptions.model_provider: cohere | generic. Auto-set from model_id family.
_confDescriptions.region: OCI region. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. (e.g. us-ashburn-1, us-chicago-1)
_confDescriptions.endpoint: OCI GenAI inference endpoint. Auto-derived from region (readonly).
_confDescriptions.compartment_id: OCI compartment OCID. OCI Console → Identity & Security → Compartments.
_confDescriptions.auth_type: Auth flavor: resource_principal (default, on-cluster), instance_principal, or api_key.
_confDescriptions.temperature: LLM sampling temperature (0.0 = deterministic, recommended for scoring).
_confDescriptions.max_tokens: Max tokens for the judge response (verdict JSON).
_confDescriptions.pass_threshold: Overall score (0-10) at or above which pass=true is returned.
_confDescriptions.max_candidate_chars: Cap on characters of candidate text fed to the judge; longer input is truncated.
_confDescriptions.max_rubric_chars: Cap on characters of rubric text; longer input is truncated.

tools.description:
Summarizes long text via map-reduce when the input exceeds a single context window; returns a single condensed summary string.

When to use: Use when the user asks to summarize, condense, TL;DR, or extract key points from a document, transcript, email thread, web page body, or aggregated tool output. Pick this tool over a raw LLM call whenever the input might exceed one model context.

How to communicate: Return the summary as-is in prose. If the input was truncated by `max_input_chars`, mention that fact in one short sentence so the user knows.

Example: user says 'summarize this 40-page transcript, focus on action items'. Call {text:'<full transcript text>', instruction:'focus on action items, group by owner'} -> return the resulting bullet summary.

schema.name: text
schema.description: The full text to summarize. Pass the raw document, transcript, or concatenated content as one string. Input longer than `max_input_chars` is truncated; input longer than `chunk_chars` triggers map-reduce.
schema.default: Meeting transcript: Alice opened by reviewing Q2 revenue... (40 pages of dialogue)
examples: Meeting transcript: Alice opened by reviewing Q2 revenue... (40 pages of dialogue)
The Treaty of Westphalia, signed in 1648, ended the Thirty Years War and established... (long article body)

schema.name: instruction
schema.description: Optional guidance shaping the summary's focus, length, or format. Example: 'focus on action items', 'one paragraph, neutral tone', 'bulleted list grouped by speaker'. Optional; omit to get a generic neutral summary.
schema.default: focus on action items, group by owner
examples: focus on action items, group by owner
bulleted key findings, no more than 7 bullets

model_provider.placeholder: Auto-set: cohere or generic based on model_id

_confDescriptions.model_id: OCI GenAI summarizer model ID. See .aidp/knowledge/oci-genai-models.md (e.g. cohere.command-a-03-2025, xai.grok-4).
_confDescriptions.model_provider: cohere | generic. Auto-set from model_id family.
_confDescriptions.region: OCI region. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. (e.g. us-ashburn-1, us-chicago-1)
_confDescriptions.endpoint: OCI GenAI inference endpoint. Auto-derived from region (readonly).
_confDescriptions.compartment_id: OCI compartment OCID. OCI Console → Identity & Security → Compartments.
_confDescriptions.auth_type: Auth flavor: resource_principal (default, on-cluster), instance_principal, or api_key.
_confDescriptions.temperature: LLM sampling temperature (0.0-1.0). Low values give more faithful summaries.
_confDescriptions.max_tokens: Max tokens for each summarization step (per-chunk and final reduce).
_confDescriptions.chunk_chars: Map-reduce chunk size in characters. Smaller = more chunks, finer-grained summarization.
_confDescriptions.max_chunks: Hard cap on number of map-reduce chunks; protects against runaway costs.
_confDescriptions.max_input_chars: Hard cap on total input characters; longer text is truncated before chunking.

tools.description:
Embeds one or more strings using an OCI GenAI embeddings model and returns a list of float vectors.

When to use: Use when the user (or another tool) needs vector representations of text — typically for semantic search, similarity scoring, clustering, or upserting into a vector index. Pick this whenever you would otherwise call an external embeddings API.

How to communicate: Report the number of vectors returned and their dimensionality (e.g. '3 vectors, dim 1024'). Do not dump the raw float arrays into the reply unless the user explicitly asks.

Example: user says 'embed these three product titles for search'. Call {texts:['Red running shoes','Blue hiking boots','Trail socks'], input_type:'search_document'} -> reply 'Embedded 3 strings into 1024-dim vectors.'

schema.name: text
schema.description: A single string to embed. Use this OR `texts`, not both. Pick `text` for one-off query embeddings (e.g. a search query); pick `texts` for batch document embedding.
schema.default: best running shoes for flat feet
examples: best running shoes for flat feet
How do I reset my password?

schema.name: texts
schema.description: A list of strings to embed in one batch. Use this OR `text`, not both. Up to `max_texts` items (Cohere caps at 96); each item longer than `max_text_chars` is handled per the `truncate` setting.
examples: Refund policy: items can be returned within 30 days.
Shipping: orders ship within 2 business days.

schema.name: input_type
schema.description: Embedding input-type hint (Cohere semantics). Use `search_document` for items being indexed, `search_query` for the user's lookup query, `classification` for label prediction inputs, `clustering` for unsupervised grouping. Optional; defaults to `search_document`.

model_provider.placeholder: Auto-set: cohere or generic based on model_id

_confDescriptions.model_id: OCI GenAI embeddings model ID (e.g. cohere.embed-english-v3.0, cohere.embed-multilingual-v3.0). See .aidp/knowledge/oci-genai-models.md.
_confDescriptions.model_provider: cohere | generic. Auto-set from model_id family.
_confDescriptions.region: OCI region. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. (e.g. us-ashburn-1, us-chicago-1)
_confDescriptions.endpoint: OCI GenAI inference endpoint. Auto-derived from region (readonly).
_confDescriptions.compartment_id: OCI compartment OCID. OCI Console → Identity & Security → Compartments.
_confDescriptions.auth_type: Auth flavor: resource_principal (default, on-cluster), instance_principal, or api_key.
_confDescriptions.input_type: Default embedding input type if not provided per-call: search_document, search_query, classification, or clustering (Cohere semantics).
_confDescriptions.max_texts: Max number of strings per embed request (Cohere limit is 96).
_confDescriptions.max_text_chars: Per-string character cap; longer strings are handled per truncate setting.
_confDescriptions.truncate: How to handle over-length strings: NONE (error), START (drop head), or END (drop tail).
